channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Littering and suspected drug-related waste under basketball court stairs, 10th June
item.description:
There is a large accumulation of litter and waste underneath the stairs next to the basketball court. The area contains numerous small bottles/vials, general rubbish, plastic bags, broken glass and other waste materials. The condition of the site is deteriorating and appears to have been left unattended for a long time. The large number of identical small bottles raises concerns that they may be related to medication or other substances and could pose a health and safety risk, particularly as this is located directly beside a public basketball court used by children and families. In addition, there is a persistent litter problem across the basketball court itself, with rubbish regularly being left on and around the playing area.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Llanover Road, Woolwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bags of Rubbish Dumped by Utility Cabinet, 10th June
item.description:
Several bags of rubbish have been left dumped on the pavement by the Utility Cabinet (by the Church). Can the local team please remove them. Thank you.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Griffin Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Unwanted Timber Dumped on Pavement, 10th June
item.description:
A stack of unwanted timber has been left dumped on the pavement outside number 65. Can the local team please remove it. Thank you.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brewery Road, Plumstead

item.title: Abandoned Fiat Doblo BV12 KVX — follow-up to ref 9493034, 10th June
item.description:
Follow-up to ref 9493034 (no action since 22 May). Fiat Doblo BV12 KVX, black. DVLA returns 'no record' for plate. 4+ weeks on courtyard at 2 Leswin Place N16 7NJ (Unit F), blocking deliveries and workshop access. Please act urgently.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Leswin Place, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: CORRECTED report — abandoned Fiat Doblo DV12 KVX — follow-up to ref 9493034, 10th June
item.description:
CORRECTED REG: DV12 KVX (prior ref 9598933 had typo). Follow-up to ref 9493034 (no action since 22 May). Fiat Doblo, black. DVLA: untaxed since 31 Jan, MOT expired 4 Mar 2026. 4+ wks at 2 Leswin Place N16 7NJ (Unit F), blocking access. Urgent.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Leswin Place, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Blocked road gully near to Indian restaurant resulting in flooding, 10th June
item.description:
Blocked roadside gully needs cleaning out. Weeds are growing in it.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Station Crescent, Blackheath

item.title: Iron shelves, 10th June
item.description:
Rusted, iron shelves dumped by green electrical cupboard on Horsa Rd

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 124, Horsa Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken, uneven paving slabs which are a dangerous trip hazard, 10th June
item.description:
The paving slabs were only recently repaired. They have been damaged yet again. They are sunken, uneven and broken. A very dangerous trip hazard. This is repeatedly happening due to adjacent building works at No. 28 and previously at No. 26. Very heavy delivery lorries repeatedly park up on the pavement instead of the road, causing the damage.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 30, Edgebury, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped iron chair lounger, 10th June
item.description:
Long lounger chair dumped on pavement

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Horsa Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flooding mess, 10th June
item.description:
After the heavy rainfall the road was flooded and after the water finally drained away the road is now left looking messy with debris and it needs sweeping

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Woodbrook Road, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Wheelchair accessibility issue – high kerb and lack of dropped kerb on Bermondsey Street, 10th June
item.description:
Today I witnessed a wheelchair user travelling independently on Bermondsey Street who was unable to access the pavement because the kerb was too high and there was no suitable dropped kerb nearby. As a result, she was forced to continue along the road while vehicles were travelling behind her, creating a potentially dangerous situation. This location appears to present an accessibility barrier for wheelchair users and others with mobility impairments. I have attached photographs showing the kerb and pavement layout and would be grateful if the council could investigate whether improvements, such as a dropped kerb or other accessibility measures, are needed to make this area safer and more accessible.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 169, Bermondsey Street, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
